text.title: Hybrid Delivery Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Hybrid Delivery methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Method MUST be assigned per component based on a documented risk profile (compliance, regulatory, novel-tech, vendor-skill); arbitrary assignment is rejected.
rule.rationale: without risk-anchored method choice, the program collapses into 'this team prefers Scrum' politics.

rule.statement: Every method change between components MUST have a named translation boundary (artefact handoff, integration point, milestone) with explicit input/output contracts.
rule.rationale: un-named boundaries cause artefacts to leak across methods and quality gates get skipped.

rule.statement: Program-level governance cadence (stage-gate review) is fixed and overrides component-level cadence at the boundary; agile components MUST report into the stage-gate cycle.
rule.rationale: drifting governance cadence kills the predictive layer that hybrid relies on.

rule.statement: Definition of Done at the COMPONENT level AND at the PROGRAM level MUST be distinct; sharing a single DoD across both is forbidden.
rule.rationale: component DoD optimises for the method; program DoD optimises for the funding gate. Conflation hides gate failures.

rule.statement: Each component's method assignment MUST cite evidence (regulatory clause, risk register entry, vendor scorecard); un-anchored assignments are rejected.
rule.rationale: agent-assigned methods without evidence drift toward 'whatever the loudest stakeholder prefers'.
